error.signature: AI assumes WhatsApp/FaceTime voice or video calls work normally in the UAE

verdict.summary: Unlicensed VoIP is a regulated (blocked) service in the UAE under the TDRA's VoIP policy: WhatsApp/FaceTime/Viber voice and video calls do not connect on UAE networks. Messaging works; calls require TDRA-licensed apps (e.g. Botim) or business tools like Teams and Zoom.

dead_ends.action: Plan to call family via WhatsApp or FaceTime from the UAE
dead_ends.why_fails: Licensed operators are required to block unlicensed VoIP traffic under TDRA regulation; voice/video calls in WhatsApp, FaceTime and Viber fail on both mobile data and most Wi-Fi networks, while text messaging works.

dead_ends.action: Use a VPN specifically to place blocked VoIP calls
dead_ends.why_fails: Using a VPN to commit an act prohibited by UAE law is itself an offence under cybercrime provisions, with heavy fines; reliability is also poor as VPN endpoints are actively blocked.

workarounds.action: Use TDRA-licensed calling apps (Botim, C'ME, Voico) or business tools (Teams, Zoom)
workarounds.how: Install a licensed VoIP app after arrival (often bundled with local SIM/eSIM plans from e& or du), or schedule calls through enterprise platforms such as Microsoft Teams and Zoom, which are permitted.
